text:
There was once an old oak tree that stood tall in its forest home. Each autumn, the leaves on its boughs would gather golden hues, painting a portrait of autumn across the land.

The tree was wise. It had witnessed many seasons unfold. Once, when it heard tales of brave knights questing for mythical jewels, it hummed happily. Another time, it listened as birds sang songs of distant lands. It chuckled as squirrels forgot to bring their nuts back.

But one dark moon, after a particularly heavy rainfall, the old tree saw something it had never seen before. A tiny, light-skinned boy was sliding down its trunk, laughing.

The boy stopped halfway down. "Won't you climb down with me?"

The tree smiled. "Perhaps," it replied. "I'm glad you're having fun."

They explored the tree's hollow interior. Alongside dusty books about wizards and mythical beasts, they found leaves that shimmered like stars. Before long, a tiny sparkle
